title: sorbus aria var. typica
intro: Sorbus aria var. typica, also known as the Whitebeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of Europe, belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Sorbus aria var. typica include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Whitebeam include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be used to make teas, syrups, and salves for topical application.
